title: Newsagents Association of NSW and the ACT - Authorisation - A91269
text: The Newsagents Association of NSW and ACT Ltd (NANA) proposes to collectively bargain on behalf of its members with NSW Lotteries in relation to the terms and conditions of agency agreements and distribution services including: commission fees; handling fees; agency application fees; support services levies; freight charges; product ordering fees; equipment hire costs; retail image design and agency subsidy and insurances; and any conduct protocols issued by NSW Lotteries. The ACCC has issued a final determination granting authorisation to NANA to collectively bargain, on behalf of its members, with NSW Lotteries. The ACCC granted interim authorisation to allow NANA to commence collective bargaining with NSW Lotteries. Interim authorisation will remain in place until the ACCC's final decision comes into effect or if the ACCC revokes interim authorisation. A copy of the ACCC's decision is available by following the link below.

title: Victorian Farmers Federation - Horticulture Group - Australian Processing Tomato Growers' Branch - Authorisation - A91270
text: Australian Processing Tomato Growers, a branch of the Victorian Farmers Federation's Horticulture Group, proposes to bargain collectively with tomato processors on behalf of 22 named tomato growers and future members. A copy of the application and submission are available by following the links below. On 24 February 2012 the ACCC issued a final determination granting authorisation for a period of five years. The scope of the conduct authorised does not extend to allowing growers who operate a tomato-processing business or have an interest in a tomato-processing business to participate in the collective bargaining activities of the group. A copy of the ACCC's determination is available by following the links below.

title: NBN Co Limited - Authorisations - A91271 - A91273
text: In August 2011 NBN Co Limited (NBN Co) sought authorisation for particular provisions of an agreement between itself and SingTel Optus Pty Ltd and other Optus entities (Optus) to migrate Optus' hybrid fibre-coaxial cable (HFC) subscribers to the national broadband network. In January 2012 NBN Co and Optus amended an aspect of this arrangement known as the 'anti-disparagement' provision. As part of this process NBN Co withdrew its August 2011 applications for authorisation (A91271-A91273) and sought authorisation for the amended agreement (A91290-A91292). A copy of NBN Co's January 2012 applications and supporting submissions provided by NBN Co and Optus is available from the ACCC's Authorisations Register.

title: Australian Writers' Guild Limited - Authorisation - A91274
text: AWG sought authorisation, on behalf of its current and future members, to collectively negotiate model agreements which can be used by current and future members of AWG when contracting with producers of either film or television. The AWG proposes negotiating the model terms of engagement with the Screen Producers Association of Australia (SPAA). The model terms of engagement will include a set of common terms relating to the price of services supplied (and the price of goods and intellectual property incidentally supplied in connection with the services) by members of AWG who choose to utilise the model terms of engagement in negotiations with producers. AWG also hopes the terms of engagement will serve as a benchmark for Screen Australia as to what constitutes "fair and reasonable conduct" when public funding recipients engage writers (as part of the Screen Australia Terms of Trade). The ACCC has issued a determination granting authorisation until 28 February 2017.

title: Abbot Point Coal Export Terminal Producers - Authorisation - A91275
text: The Applicants propose to: (i) collectively discuss and negotiate the terms and conditions, including price, under which access to Below Rail Infrastructure for the purpose of transporting coal to the Abbot Point Coal Terminal, will be acquired from QR Network Pty Ltd (QR Network); (ii) collectively discuss matters relating to those discussions and negotiations; and (iii) enter into and give effect to contracts, arrangements or understandings with QR Network (or any successor or asignee) containing common terms and conditions, including price, upon which access to the Below Rail Infrastructure will be acquired. Below Rail Infrastructure refers to all activities associated with the provision and management of rail infrastructure, including the construction, maintenance and renewal of rail infrastructure assets. The ACCC has issued a determination granting authorisation until 10 March 2027.

title: CALMS Ltd - Revocation & Substitution - A91276
text: CALMS lodged an application for revocation of A91092 and substitution of new authorisation A91276 to agree and set capped fees for the provision of after hours primary medical in the Australian Capital Territory. CALMS requested interim authorisation from the period its current authorisation expires (the 31 October 2011). Interim authorisation was granted by the ACCC on 20 October 2011. On 16 December 2011, the ACCC issued the draft determination which proposed to grant CALMS authorisation for ten years. On 27 February 2012 the ACCC re-authorised the conduct for 10 years and released its final determination.

title: Dudgeon Point Coal Export Terminal Producers (QR Network Pty Ltd) - Authorisation - A91278
text: The Applicants seek authorisation to: (i) collectively discuss and negotiate the terms and conditions, including price, under which access to Below Rail Infrastructure for the purpose of transporting coal to the Dudgeon Point Terminal, will be acquired from QR Network Pty Ltd (QR Network); (ii) collectively discuss matters relating to those discussions and negotiations; and (iii) enter into and give effect to contracts, arrangements or understandings with QR Network (or any successor or asignee) containing common terms and conditions, including price, upon which access to the Below Rail Infrastructure will be acquired. Below Rail Infrastructure refers to all activities associated with the provision and management of rail infrastructure, including the construction, maintenance and renewal of rail infrastructure assets. The ACCC has issued a determination granting authorisation until 10 March 2027.

title: Dudgeon Point Coal Export Terminal Producers (Dudgeon Point Project Management Pty Ltd) - Authorisation - A91277
text: The Applicants seek authorisation to: (i) collectively discuss and negotiate with Dudgeon Point Project Management Pty Ltd (DPPM), the terms and conditions, including price, for the development of and access to Port Infrastructure at the Dudgeon Point Terminal; (ii) collectively discuss matters relating to those discussions and negotiations; and (iii) enter into and give effect to contracts, arrangements or understandings with DPPM (or any successor or asignee) containing consistent terms and conditions, including price, upon which access to the Port Infrastructure will be acquired. Port Infrastructure refers to all activities associated with loading and unloading coal onto vessels for export, including stockpiling, reclaiming, conveying and blending coal at the Terminal. The ACCC granted interim authorisation to the Applicants on 26 October 2011 to allow them to collectively negotiate but not enter into contracts with DPPM. The ACCC issued a draft determination proposing to grant authorisation for 15 years on 14 December 2011 The ACCC has issued a determination granting authorisation until 10 March 2027.

title: Retirees WA (Inc) - Revocation & Substitution - A91279
text: Retirees WA (Inc)(RWA) has sought renewal of its authorisation (A91015) allowing for reduced cost pre-paid funerals to be agreed to between the RWA and members of the Western Australian Funeral Directors' Association (WAFDA members) and between RWA and non-WAFDA funeral directors (Independent Funeral Directors). On 16 February 2012, the ACCC proposed to grant authorisation for ten years. RWA also requested interim authorisation from the period its current authorisation expires (29 March 2012). Interim authorisation was granted by the ACCC and this decision was also released on 16 February 2012. The ACCC has issued a determination granting authorisation until 4 May 2022. A copy of the application, submissions and ACCC decisions are available by following the links below.

title: The Real Estate Institute of Western Australia (Inc) (REIWA) - Revocation & Substitution - A91280
text: The Real Estate Institute of Western Australia (Inc) (REIWA) lodged an application for revocation and substitution of authorisation A91026 which originally provided for its Articles, Members' Codes of Practice, Auction Code of Conduct, Multiple Listing Service By-Laws and Standard Exclusive Agency Agreements (together, REIWA's membership framework). On 19 April 2012 the ACCC issued a final determination granting conditional authorisation in respect of REIWA's membership framework for five years. A copy of the ACCC's determination is available by following the links below.

title: Australian Payments Clearing Association Limited - Revocation and Substitution - A91281, A91282 & A91283
text: The Australian Payments Clearing Association Ltd (APCA) has lodged an application for revocation of authorisations A91016 to A91018 in relation to the High Value Clearing System (HVCS) Regulations and Procedures (previously authorised on 14 February 2007) and substitution of new authorisations in relation to suspension and termination of HVCS membership and the requirement that members join SWIFT (relevant provisions). On 25 January 2012 the ACCC granted interim authorisation. On 2 May 2012 the ACCC issued a final determination granting authorisation in respect of the relevant provisions for 10 years. A copy of the ACCC's determination is available by following the links below.

title: Homemakers South Ltd t/a Homemakers Furniture & Sleepzone Bedding - Authorisation - A91284
text: Homemakers South Limited T/A Homemakers Furniture & Sleepzone Bedding (Homemakers) seeks authorisation on behalf of its members to collectively negotiate with suppliers for the supply agreements of furniture, bedding and related stock, advertising and marketing services and any commercial arrangements ancillary to those matters. Homemakers is a buying, advertising and marketing group and also operates a small warehouse to sell clearance stock. It acts on behalf of independent furniture retail outlets in Victoria, New South Wales, South Australia, Tasmania and Western Australia. The ACCC has issued a final determination grant ing authorisation until 31 May 2017.

title: Viscopy Limited & Copyright Agency Limited - Authorisations - A91285 & A91286
text: The Applicants have entered into a Service Agreement under which Copyright Agency will provide various services to Viscopy including the management and administration of day-to-day ooperations, and seek authorisation to: jointly discuss and negotiate common terms and conditions including fees and scope of services, make and give effect to contracts arrangements and understandings between the applicants, and continue to give effect jointly to the provisions of such agreements.

title: Virgin Australia and Skywest Airlines - Authorisations - A91287 & A91288
text: On 8 December 2011 Virgin Australia and Skywest Airlines applied for authorisation to make, and give effect to, their Corporate Alliance Framework Agreement, Frequent Flyer and Lounge Agreement and Joint Sales Agreement (together the Corporate Alliance) for the provision of commercial passenger airline services to corporate customers. The Corporate Alliance will be limited to offering corporate customers a suite of charter and domestic and international regular public transport air services. On 25 January the ACCC granted interim authorisation. Interim authorisation will remain in place until the date that the ACCC's final determination comes into effect or until interim authorisation is revoked. On 10 May 2012 the ACCC issued a determination granting authorisation in respect of the Corporate Alliance for five years until 1 June 2017. A copy of the determination is available by following the links below.

title: Hunter Resource Recovery - Authorisation - A91289
text: On 21 December 2011, Hunter Resource Recovery lodged an application for authorisation for a tender process under which HRR will invite tenders on behalf of the Cessnock City Council, Lake Macquarie City Council, Maitland City Council and Singleton Council (the Councils) for a joint recycling contract for the collection, sorting and disposal of dry recyclables collected from residential and commercial clients of the Councils. HRR proposes that a single tenderer will be selected by the Councils through the collective tender process. HRR will contract with the successful tenderer as delegate and trustee of the Councils for a period of 10 years with the option of 2x1 year extensions. On 25 January the ACCC granted interim authorisation. Interim authorisation commenced immediately and remained in place until the date that the ACCC's final determination came into effect. On 1 March 2012, the ACCC issued a draft determination proposing to grant authorisation in respect of the proposed arrangements for twelve years. On 12 April 2012 the ACCC authorised the conduct for 12 years (until 4 May 2024) and released its final determination. A copy of the application, submissions and ACCC decisions are available by following the links below.
